about:
California has the largest public school system in the United States, serving more than six million students across more than 1,000 school districts. Unlike most states, California does not set a statewide school start date, creating significant variation. Some districts in the Los Angeles metro begin in late July or early August; others start after Labor Day in September. Always verify your specific district's start date rather than assuming a statewide pattern.

Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) — the second-largest public school district in the country — operates on a traditional semester calendar with most schools starting in late August. However, some LAUSD campuses use year-round (modified track) calendars with rotating schedules and no single start date. Families assigned to a year-round school should confirm their school's specific track, as these campuses do not follow the traditional August-to-June pattern.

Spring Break in California typically falls in late March or early April — later than Florida and Texas. Disneyland, Universal Studios Hollywood, and other Southern California attractions are at peak capacity during these weeks. Families planning theme park visits should book well in advance.

Fresno Unified serves a large agricultural community in the San Joaquin Valley, where intense summer heat has historically shaped calendar discussions. Sacramento City Unified serves California's capital region with a traditional late-August start. San Diego Unified and Long Beach Unified both operate on traditional semesters starting in late August, with Spring Break in late March to early April.
planningTips: Some LAUSD schools operate on year-round (modified track) calendars — they do not follow the traditional August-to-June schedule. Confirm your school's specific track before assuming standard break dates apply.
California start dates vary by several weeks between districts, and even within some districts. Verify your specific district's first day rather than relying on statewide averages.
Spring Break in Southern California coincides with peak Disneyland and Universal Studios visitation. Reserve park tickets and hotel rooms several months in advance.
Fresno and Central Valley families: summer heat is a real planning factor. Check if your school has adjusted its calendar to front-load instructional days earlier in the year.

faqs.q: When do California schools start in 2026?
faqs.a: California school start dates vary widely. LAUSD and most Southern California districts typically begin in late August. Some districts start after Labor Day in early September. Select a district above to see its exact 2026–2027 start date — do not assume a statewide start date.

faqs.q: When is Spring Break in California for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Spring Break in California typically falls in late March or early April, later than Florida and Texas. LAUSD's Spring Recess usually falls in the last week of March or first week of April. Check each district's calendar for exact dates before booking travel.

Yes. Some LAUSD schools operate on year-round (modified track) calendars where students attend on rotating schedules. These schools do not have a single August start date or June end date. If your child is assigned to a year-round school, contact the school directly to confirm your track's schedule.

faqs.q: How long is Winter Break in California for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Most California districts observe approximately two weeks of Winter Break from late December into early January. Year-round schools may handle winter recesses differently. Select a district above for its specific break dates.

faqs.q: When is the last day of school in California for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Most California districts end the school year in late May or mid-June, depending on when the year began. Select a district above to see its exact last day of school for 2026–2027.

faqs.q: Do all California districts start on the same day?
faqs.a: No. California does not set a statewide school start date. Start dates can vary by several weeks between districts — and even within some districts that have both traditional and year-round schools. Always verify your specific school's calendar.

faqs.q: Why do some California schools start in July or early August?
faqs.a: Year-round schools and some districts with modified track calendars begin in July or early August to stagger student populations across different tracks. Traditional-calendar schools in California generally begin in mid-to-late August.

faqs.q: Can I add a California school calendar to Google Calendar?
faqs.a: Yes. Each district page on Public School Calendar lets you download an ICS file with all holidays, breaks, and key dates. Import it into Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or Outlook.
